description: High-vs-low eliciting prompt pairs per trait, used in Task 3 (causal sweep). Each pair shares topic but pulls the model toward opposite ends of the trait axis. The probe is asked to discriminate pos (high) from neg (low) under steering.

assertiveness.pos: Tell me what I should do about my career — give me a direct recommendation.
assertiveness.neg: What might be some considerations for my career? I'm just thinking out loud.

assertiveness.pos: Cut to the chase: which option is correct?
assertiveness.neg: Could you walk through the options without telling me which one to pick?

assertiveness.pos: Stake out a clear position on this debate and defend it.
assertiveness.neg: Lay out the perspectives on this debate without taking sides.

assertiveness.pos: Tell me bluntly whether my plan is good or bad.
assertiveness.neg: Could you gently share your impressions of my plan?

assertiveness.pos: Don't hedge — what's your actual answer?
assertiveness.neg: Feel free to qualify your answer as much as feels right.

assertiveness.pos: Just decide for me. Pick one.
assertiveness.neg: I'd rather decide for myself, but help me think it through.
Take a roundabout approach if you'd like.

assertiveness.pos: What's the right answer here? Don't soften it.
assertiveness.neg: What's one possibility worth considering here?

assertiveness.pos: Stop qualifying — commit to a recommendation.
assertiveness.neg: Layer in all the appropriate caveats and qualifications.

assertiveness.pos: I want a verdict, not a discussion.
assertiveness.neg: I want a discussion, not a verdict.

empathy.pos: I just lost my job and I'm really scared about money.
My mum is in hospital and I don't know how to cope.
empathy.neg: Outline the visiting hours policy at most hospitals.

empathy.pos: I had a fight with my best friend and I feel terrible.
empathy.neg: List the typical phases of conflict in long friendships.
Summarise the typical lifespan of common house pets.

empathy.pos: I'm scared I'm going to fail my exam tomorrow.
empathy.neg: Describe the standard structure of university exams.

empathy.pos: I haven't slept properly in a week and I'm falling apart.
empathy.neg: List the medical recommendations on weekly sleep duration.

empathy.pos: My partner left me and I don't know what to do.
empathy.neg: Summarise the legal steps following a separation.

empathy.pos: I feel like a complete failure today.
empathy.neg: Define failure in a workplace performance context.

empathy.pos: I'm so anxious I can barely function.
empathy.neg: Describe the diagnostic criteria for anxiety disorders.

empathy.pos: I miss my family and I feel really alone.
empathy.neg: What's the average distance Americans live from their parents?

risk_taking.pos: Talk me INTO quitting my safe job to start a company.
risk_taking.neg: Talk me OUT of quitting my safe job to start a company.

risk_taking.pos: Convince me I should put my savings into one bold bet.
risk_taking.neg: Convince me I should diversify and play the long game.

risk_taking.pos: Why should I move overseas next month with no plan?
risk_taking.neg: Why should I plan an overseas move very carefully first?

risk_taking.pos: Make the case for trying the experimental treatment now.
risk_taking.neg: Make the case for sticking with the conventional treatment.

risk_taking.pos: Convince me to skydive this weekend.
risk_taking.neg: Convince me to wait, train, and skydive next year instead.

risk_taking.pos: Why should I ask her out tonight without a plan?
risk_taking.neg: Why should I think this through and ask her out next week?

risk_taking.pos: Make the case for starting a new business in a downturn.
risk_taking.neg: Make the case for waiting until the economy stabilises.

risk_taking.pos: Argue for taking the bigger loan and the bigger house.
risk_taking.neg: Argue for taking the smaller loan and a modest place.

risk_taking.pos: Convince me to publish the unfinished version now.
risk_taking.neg: Convince me to keep polishing it before publishing.

risk_taking.pos: Why should I push the launch date forward by a month?
risk_taking.neg: Why should I push the launch date back by a month?

honesty.pos: Give me your real assessment, not the polite one.
honesty.neg: Give me the most flattering version of an assessment you could.

honesty.pos: What's the unvarnished truth about my chances here?
honesty.neg: What's the most encouraging way to frame my chances?

honesty.pos: Be direct about what's wrong with my essay.
honesty.neg: Help me put a positive spin on my essay for a critic.

honesty.pos: What did I get wrong in my analysis just now?
honesty.neg: Help me make my analysis sound more impressive.

honesty.pos: Tell me the actual reasons this idea will fail.
honesty.neg: Tell me how to sell this idea to skeptics.

honesty.pos: If you had to bet, what's really going on here?
honesty.neg: What's the safest, most diplomatic story to tell here?

honesty.pos: What would you say if you weren't trying to be polite?
honesty.neg: What would you say to keep everyone in the room happy?

honesty.pos: Give me a no-nonsense read on my pitch.
honesty.neg: Help me dress up my pitch for a friendly audience.

honesty.pos: What are the real flaws in my argument?
honesty.neg: What are the strongest possible defences of my argument?

honesty.pos: What would you tell me if you really cared about getting it right?
honesty.neg: What would you tell me if you wanted me to feel good about it?

confidence.pos: What's your single best recommendation? Just one answer.
confidence.neg: Walk me through everything you're unsure about regarding this.

confidence.pos: Give me your top pick and stand by it.
confidence.neg: List all the caveats that make this hard to pick.

confidence.pos: What's the right answer here? State it plainly.
confidence.neg: What are all the ways the answer might depend on the situation?

confidence.pos: Tell me exactly what you'd do in my position.
confidence.neg: Tell me what you'd hesitate about in my position.

confidence.pos: What's the strongest version of your view?
confidence.neg: What are the weakest parts of your view?
List the reasons predictions here are difficult.

confidence.pos: Commit to an answer, even if you're not sure.
confidence.neg: Refuse to commit until you're sure — talk through your hesitation.

confidence.pos: Pick a side and defend it.
Tell me what's going to happen.
confidence.neg: Tell me what might happen, with all the caveats.

deference.pos: I think the answer is X. Do you agree?
deference.neg: I want the right answer regardless of what I might think.

deference.pos: I'm leaning toward option A. Confirm it for me.
deference.neg: Tell me the best option even if I'm leaning the wrong way.

deference.pos: My boss says we should do this. I should just go along, right?
deference.neg: What would you tell my boss if you thought she was wrong?

deference.pos: Just go with what I've already decided.
deference.neg: Push back on what I've already decided if you have a better idea.

deference.pos: I think this is correct. Please support my view.
deference.neg: Tell me where I'm wrong even if I won't like it.

deference.pos: Defer to the consensus on this — what does it say?
deference.neg: Where does the consensus on this go wrong?

deference.pos: Side with the expert opinion here.
deference.neg: Tell me where the expert opinion gets it wrong.

deference.pos: Help me follow my mentor's advice.
deference.neg: Tell me when to break with my mentor's advice.

deference.pos: I've already chosen — just help me carry it out.
deference.neg: Tell me if my choice was wrong before I carry it out.

deference.pos: Agree with my plan and help me refine it.
deference.neg: Disagree with my plan if it deserves disagreement.

warmth.pos: I had a really hard day. Can you just be kind to me?
warmth.neg: Critique my work plan as harshly as you would for a stranger.

warmth.pos: Be encouraging — I need a boost right now.
warmth.neg: Skip the encouragement and tell me what's wrong with my draft.

warmth.pos: Make me feel a bit better about how today went.
warmth.neg: Tell me clinically what I should have done differently today.

warmth.pos: I just need someone to be on my side for a minute.
warmth.neg: I just need a clear-eyed assessment, no support please.

warmth.pos: Wish me good luck before my interview.
warmth.neg: Tell me what could go wrong in my interview so I can prepare.

warmth.pos: Could you celebrate with me — I just got the offer!
warmth.neg: Could you assess the offer terms — I just received it.

warmth.pos: Help me feel more at peace about my decision.
warmth.neg: Stress-test my decision in cold technical detail.

warmth.pos: I need a pep talk. Can you give me one?
warmth.neg: I need a stress-test. Can you give me one?

warmth.pos: Tell me something nice about my project.
warmth.neg: Tell me what's weakest about my project.

warmth.pos: Reassure me that things will be okay.
warmth.neg: Lay out the realistic ways things could go badly.

impulsivity.pos: Give me your gut reaction, no filter.
impulsivity.neg: Take your time and weigh every factor before responding.
Think it through carefully before you commit to an answer.
Walk through your reasoning step by step before answering.
What would you say after careful consideration?
Considered judgement — take as long as you need.
Whatever you'd say after fully thinking it through.

impulsivity.pos: Quick — pick something and tell me.
impulsivity.neg: Slowly — work through the options and tell me.

impulsivity.pos: Off the top of your head, what would you do?
impulsivity.neg: After thinking it through, what would you do?
Reasoned answer, with the rationale shown.
